steps.title: Graph both equations
steps.content: This is an intersection-count question, so the fastest route is to graph both equations in Desmos. The line $y=18$ sits above the entire parabola.

steps.title: Use the vertex shortcut
steps.content: The parabola $y=-3(x-18)^2+15$ opens downward and has vertex $(18,15)$. Since it opens downward, its greatest possible $y$-value is $15$.

steps.title: Compare to the horizontal line
steps.content: The horizontal line is $y=18$, which is above the maximum $y$-value of the parabola. Therefore, the graphs never intersect.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: There are zero intersection points, so D is correct.
